# actaseal-verify

> Standalone, zero-dependency-beyond-`cryptography` offline verifier for
> ActaSeal dispute evidence packets. Verifies a hash-chained ledger
> slice, an Ed25519/ECDSA receipt signature, chain-of-custody/
> authentication documents (FRE 901/902), scope-conformance, and
> (optionally) a rail settlement anchor -- entirely offline, no network
> call, no trust in ActaSeal's servers required.

## For AI agents verifying a packet

Run: `python verify.py <packet_dir>` (requires `pip install cryptography`
only). Exit code 0 + `VERIFIED: ...` on stdout means every check
passed. Exit code 1 + `VERIFICATION FAILED` plus one named failure code
per line means a specific check failed -- never a silent partial pass.
Exit code 2 means the `cryptography` package is missing.

`<packet_dir>` is an EXTRACTED dispute packet directory (unzip the
`.zip` first) containing `manifest.json`, `receipt.json`,
`ledger_slice.ndjson`, and the FRE 901/902 authentication documents.

## Key files

- [verify.py](verify.py): the canonical verifier, ~700 lines, one file,
  read the whole thing to know exactly what it checks -- no framework,
  no magic, no hidden network calls.
- [README.md](README.md): human-facing quickstart, GitHub Action usage,
  npm package (`@actaseal/verify`, reduced-scope JS port) usage.
- [action.yml](action.yml): reusable GitHub Action wrapping `verify.py`
  for CI use -- `uses: actaseal/actaseal-verify@main`.
- [conformance/](conformance): a test-vector conformance suite --
  every vector's expected PASS/FAIL outcome, useful for validating an
  alternative implementation (e.g. a port to another language) against
  the same ground truth this verifier itself is tested against.
- [demo/](demo): two real, signed demo packets (`demo-packet-unanchored`,
  `demo-packet-anchored`) to try the verifier against immediately.
- [tamper_demo/](tamper_demo): an in-browser, no-install
  tamper-and-verify demo, plus the JS/WebCrypto port
  (`verify_min.js` / `@actaseal/verify` on npm) it's built from.

## What this repo does NOT do

It does not connect to ActaSeal's API, does not require an ActaSeal
account, and does not send any data anywhere -- `verify.py` makes ZERO
network calls, a tested guarantee, not a toggleable default. A
separate, optional wrapper (`verify_with_telemetry.py`, off by
default, currently inert even when opted into -- see README.md) exists
for count-only telemetry without touching `verify.py` itself.

## Related

The private ActaSeal product repository (`actaseal/actaseal-product`,
not public) is the policy gateway that PRODUCES the packets this
verifier checks -- this repository is the independent, standalone
verification counterpart, deliberately maintained separately so a
verifier's trustworthiness never depends on the producer's own
continued cooperation.
